desc:
Weary travellers in need of rest may arrange for a private room here. Most guest
chambers are small, yet comfortable, and offer a relaxing view of the Bruinen as
it rolls past Rivendell.
name: Imladris Private Guest Rooms

desc:
You feel a spiritual growth within yourself as you enter. At this guild the
master specializes in channeling the god's will towards healing, but also knows
many other prayers.
name: Cleric Guild

desc:
The spell masters of Rivendell are here at all hours to keep up with the demand
for magical knowledge. Here one can learn a broad selection of mage spells, from
elves who have had centuries to hone their talents.
name: Mage Guild

desc:
The stairs continue up to the third floor and down to the ground floor. A
carpeted hallway extends east from here, while a wide terrace of white stone,
probably marble, opens to the south. A white, graceful archway leads towards
another section of the Last Homely Home in the north.
name: Stairway

desc:
The spacious hallway runs east to west. More portraits, this time of the
Dunedain Kings, are in view on the walls; Elendil, and his remarkable son,
Isildur.
name: Grand Hallway

desc:
The spacious hallway runs east to west. On the red velvet-covered walls you
notice some portraits of great elves who still remain in Middle-earth today;
Glorfindel, a warrior who survived countless battles and trials, Galadriel,
queen of the elves of Lorien, and Elrond, master of Rivendell.
name: Grand Hallway

desc:
You marvel at the vast collection of tomes, books, and scrolls in this room. You
can often find the more studious inhabitants here absorbing lore. There is a
large sign hanging on the south wall.
name: The Great Library

desc:
This room is intended as a waiting room for persons who will meet with Elrond in
the Council Chamber to the north. There are a lot of comfortable chairs, and
desks to organise documents on. You see a wooden sign with large letters carved
into it near the door.
name: Council Antechamber

desc:
This room is dominated by a long table that extends almost the entire length of
the room. The table is covered with a scrumptious looking spread and there are
many chairs to sit in. Every now and then someone will walk in and take some
food to eat, and it is quickly replaced by the kitchen staff.
name: Dining Hall

desc:
This giant-sized room takes up most of the top floor of the house. The ceiling
is formed into a dome of a glassy material which is impossible to break, yet
perfectly clear to gaze through. Although there is not much to see of the
landscape from here, the stars of the night sky come into full view, seemingly
better than even under the clearest of weather conditions.
name: Observatory

desc:
The aroma of gourmet cooking wafts through the air. You see several ovens and
huge kettles, along with an impressive collection of pots and utensils hanging
from racks near the ceiling. Cooks work here almost incessantly to turn
Rivendell's bountiful harvest into delicious food, and it looks like they truly
enjoy their labours.
name: Kitchen

desc:
This ornate staircase leads up to the Royal Suites, wherein reside Elrond and
Arwen. A guard is usually posted here to politely guide guests to the public
areas of Rivendell.
name: Private Stair

desc:
This is the famous council room where the Istari and other important persons
meet with Elrond to discuss pressing matters. A long table dominates the
furniture along with a few chairs. When this room is not in use by the council,
Elrond welcomes all who have need to use these facilities.
name: The Council Chamber

desc:
This is a nice place to take a breath of fresh air during extended council
meetings. A lovely view opens from here to the valley, with the south fork of
the Bruinen extending off into the Misty Mountains, which seem to loom right in
front of you. You can see some very tall peaks that break through the clouds,
and many that have snow and ice on them.
name: Patio

desc:
There is a small chair standing infront of the fire. It looks like a nice chair
to relax in and close enough to the fire so there wouldn't be any problem to
keep warm. On the walls there is a lot of art hanging, it makes the room look
friendly. Most of the art is hand made paintings of flowers. But one off the
paintings looks different, it's something written all over the painting.
name: Magnolia's Resting Place

desc:
You find yourself in an entrance to a building, here the floor is made of dirt.
You have to brush of your shoes before you go any futher so you dont drag any
dirt into the inner rooms of the house. In the south and north the house
continues. A large warning sign stands here.
name: Entrance

desc:
The ceiling and walls of this room are covered in a shimmering white stone of
unknown origin, broken only by occasional paintings. The paintings seem old and
fragile, depicting ancient events of Elven lore. High bookshelves follow the
walls from east to west filled with tomes of days past. Near the northern wall a
large cauldron is placed, suitable for experiments which have been known to
billow thick plumes of harmless smoke out into the main hallways of Rivendell
that lie to the west. A simple door leading to a bedchamber adorns the north
wall.
name: White Hall

desc:
Separated by an oakdoor from the rest of Rivendell, this strange house is
suffocated by smoke-filled air. To the east, further into the house, there is
even more smoke. On the wall there are several pictures showing groups of people
standing beneath a cloud-filled sky. Under one of these pictures there is a
bronze sign. The sign is ancient and dirty making it nearly illegible. However,
a few symbols on the sign, N^m'r C^st'd, can be discerned.
name: A Hallway

desc:
This cubicle allows two individuals to engage in private discussion. The walls
are plain wooden panels, void of any ornaments. The council chamber can be
glimpsed south of here.
name: Private Cubicle

desc:
This stairwell is made from a very fine oakwood. The grain shows both strength
and elegance. Both beautiful and fine carvings were added to the wooden walls
that line the stairwell long ago. The floor of the stairwell consists of solid
marble plates. The stairs connect the Modest Temple above, with the small but
well built home below.
name: Stairwell

desc:
Along the walls of this chamber many elegantly decorated chests have been
placed. A dazzling display of light seem to originate from the chests, making
their details difficult to distinguish. To the east a curved wooden stairway
leads up while to the west an bright light shines.
name: Alvedon's Treasury

desc:
As you enter this room the heat from the fireplace provides warmth that is
otherwise absent from this cold dreary home. A huge glowing table shaped as a
circle stands in the middle of the room. Two big chairs that like look miniature
thrones have been placed next to the table on opposite sides of each other. In
the eastern portion of this room a bed can be seen.
name: Alvedon's Resting Room

desc:
A narrow corridor leading south, partially lit by the doorkeep. The only choices
are heading back down to Imladris, or exploring the prayer room ahead. A small
draft also blows through this small hallway.
name: Narrow Corridor

desc:
Here, spiritual growth, above all else, is practiced. This is a room usually
shrouded in silence. The only noises are those of the northern grumblings, and
the barely audible sounds of turning pages from the south.
name: Prayer Room

desc:
This was at one time a library full of texts and lores of the ages. Now it is a
place of study and rest for the weary Priest. A steward is posted here to keep
the place tidy.
name: Converted Library

desc:
The balcony here connects the guest rooms that lie both west and east. To the
north is a wooden door, leading to similar guest quarters. Overlooking the
Bruinen, the balcony offers an expansive view southward. Some vines spiral their
way around the posts supporting the balcony's edge, almost as if trying to mimic
the elven carvings that cover other parts of the posts.
name: Balcony over the River

desc:
The balcony continues east and west, giving easy access to several doors that
line its northern side. The paved white stones underfoot form a simple and
graceful pattern. Opposite the doorway that lies to the north, the southern side
of the balcony opens up giving a wide view over the River Bruinen, its water
glistening.
name: Balcony over the River

desc:
Off the doorway at the north, the balcony continues in an east-westerly manner.
At some places its southern supporting posts are covered with entwining vines,
their green leaves trying to overwhelm the white stone. The sound of rushing
water draws attention to the south, where the River Bruinen can be seen flowing
below.
name: Balcony over the River

desc:
The waters of the Bruinen softly flow below the southern side of the balcony as
the white stone-laid walkway continues on its way to east and west. Its simple
yet elegant design does not include many complex decorations, the carvings on
the balcony posts and the lively vines seem to create an almost artistic
harmony.
name: Balcony over the River

desc:
The white stone balcony comes to a small corner as it turns both to the west and
north. Westwards one can catch a glimpse of the many doorways that line the
inner edge of the balcony, while to the north the balcony continues, leading
passers-by towards the library. Down below flows the River Bruinen, the bubbly
murmur of its waters are well heard even here.
name: Balcony over the River

desc:
The stone-laid white balcony follows the doorways that lead to several guest
rooms. Southwards the balcony forms a corner overlooking the river, with the
vines that circle the posts, hanging over the rim. To the west lies a doorway,
its surface lit up during each dawn as the sun peaks its first rays over the
eastern horizon.
name: Balcony of the Sunrise

desc:
The stone balcony ends here, as further on westwards lies the library of
Rivendell. Looking towards south, one can still clearly see the doors that line
the balcony, leading into the guest rooms. The faint sound of rushing water can
be heard from the same direction, as it travels up to the walkway from the River
Bruinen which lies beneath.
name: Balcony of the Sunrise

desc:
The dark blue walls and the skyblue ceiling of this room are merging together.
The floor is made of dark oaken wood and it is painted black. A fireplace has
been set in the centre of the room. A cosy single bed has been placed alongside
the wall opposite the oakdoor. An enchanted painting of Domy adorns the wall
above the bed. The painting glows faintly and makes the room to glitter.
name: Domy's Living Quarters

desc:
The floor is made of pine boards, that have been treated with various natural
oils, to make it white. Two five-foot high windows let in fresh air and the
colours of Rivendell. The light walls and floor create a contrast with the
furniture that has been carved of ebony.
name: Mithrel's Patio

desc:
The noises of the outside world are left outside the walls of this sanctum, save
for visitors and the occasional speech being made in the library on the other
side of the door. Throughout this peaceful place, however, a tangible
watchfulness lies heavy in the air. A soft yet distinct scent seems to spread
from one of the small boxes found in what seems to be this wizard's idea of
ordered chaos. A large window dominates the eastern wall. Below it stands a
chair and wooden table upon which lie several documents. A wooden bookshelf has
been placed beside the table, where it seems to have been left to gather dust
over time, even more dust than is seen on the dark wooden floor. Several
paintings hang on the western wall.
name: Galadon's Sanctum

desc:
This modest room has walls made of dark oaken wood and a high ceiling painted a
pale white. A long narrow bed has been placed alongside the wall opposite the
large oaken door in the western wall. A desk has been set beneath one of the
room's large windows. Some paintings of local wildlife, including one of a group
of rabbits, adorn the inner walls. From the doorway and the nearby windows one
has an excellent view of the enchanted valley of Rivendell.
name: Alderik's Chamber

desc:
This small cottage is hedged by vines on all sides. Well-tended, they seem to be
carefully grown by local inhabitants. The cottage itself is made of wooden
beams, horizontally placed one on top of each other. The roof is constructed of
grey slates, supported by thick wooden pillars. A small window beside the door
allows one see into the southern vineyards. To the east a small gap provides
access to a snuggery.
name: Living Room

desc:
This small, secluded room is covered with furs of different animals, making it
look more humble than other elven dwellings. A couple of windows allow one to
take in the view of the surrounding lands. This room, completely separated from
the rest of the cottage, may only be accessed by a small gap in the western
wall.
name: Snuggery

desc:
This old and disused cellar holds a murky smell. Debris and parts of broken
tools, armour and weapons lie scattered on the earthen floor. Brown water
trickles down the moist walls.
name: A Dark Cellar

desc:
This small wooden building is made up of a single room. A narrow bed stands in
the north-west corner of the room, and a large fireplace rests in the opposite
corner. A square table stands at the window on the southern wall, surrounded by
four wooden chairs.
name: A Small Wooden Building

desc:
This abandoned cellar room is filled with broken things; weapons, gardening
tools, armour, blacksmith's tools. Many of the items are so wrecked there is no
chance of knowing what they once were. The earthen floor is a little wet, as are
the walls.
name: A Storage Room

desc:
Oak panelling lines the walls of this room, while wide slabs of thin marble make
up the sturdy floor and lead out to the west where they join up with a large
marble balcony. There are several chairs and a sofa around the room, good for
large gatherings of friends. An arched doorway covered by a silk curtain leads
south to the bedroom. Through a window to the east, a gazebo can be seen in a
small grassy clearing.
name: Magica's Relaxation Room

desc:
You find yourself at a beautiful quiet place. This little peaceful Gazebo in the
forest is surrounded by great, ancient trees and magic seems to fill the air.
There seems to be a strong protective spell here, making this space feel safe
and comfortable. The grass and the leaves of the trees are fresh and green,
sparkling like living emeralds. The sky above is beautiful and clear - so calm
that one wonders if it ever changes, even when there are storms around. The
shadows of the trees provide privacy for those who seek it. It is obvious that
the mistress of this place and nature cherish each other very much.
name: Magica's Beautiful Gazebo

desc:
You find yourself on a large balcony made of marble. Thick columns extend from
the floor in a semicircle, forming a protective railing topped by solid oak to
prevent one from falling off into the courtyard below. The balcony affords an
impressive view of the Imladris valley, its inhabitants and the many forms of
wildlife that roam free in the area. A wide bench sits near the railing,
suitable for relaxation.
name: Balcony Overlooking Rivendell

desc:
The smell of roses permeates the room, accented by the soft glow of a few
candles which sit on top of a large dresser. Pale marble walls display a few
paintings, one of which is a prominent oil print of a graceful dove in
mid-flight. A simple vanity lies against the back wall next to the entrance to a
walk-in closet. On the west side sits a large four-poster bed with embroidered,
silken pillows adorning it, the biggest piece of furniture in the room. A screen
of royal blue silk hangs down from the posts. Another silk curtain hangs
underneath an arched doorway which leads to the north.
name: Magica's Bedroom

desc:
A circular council chamber, warmed by a large forge-like fireplace. In the
centre of the room a majestic, octagonal table is placed, circled by mahogany
chairs. Gem-inlaid lamps light the room from far above.
name: Dwarven Council

desc:
This magnificent and spacious room has the air of a meticulously maintained
museum. To the right there are intricate paintings of fair Beleriand that now
lies under the sea. To the left are paintings of the realm of Hithlum. Hanging
on the remaining walls are the weapons of Telessars' fallen enemies, gathered
from battles he has fought for as long as he can remember.
name: Telessar's Lounge

desc:
The high, arched ceiling of the room is covered with carvings depicting many of
the great battles of ages long forgotten. Light entering from several windows
illuminate the room with the colours of blood and war. The resident of the room
seems to be haunted by sacrifices that his people have made through the ages.
name: Hall of the Sunrise

desc:
You find yourself standing inside a well lit study of some sort, books and
assorted tomes of information are neatly placed on oaken bookshelving which
adorns the walls. There is a bed in the corner, as well as a solid desk with
various writing utensils. Polished wooden framed windows overlook the activity
in the courtyard below.
name: Orodreth's Private Bedroom

desc:
This dwelling is sparse with the functional kind of furniture of a person who
does not pay much attention to art or wealth. The ceiling is arched and high and
the walls still exhibit the familiar colour of the wood that was used to build
the Last Homely Home. A rather large tile stove for heating purposes is placed
in the southwest corner of the room, between the small doorways that lead into
the bedchamber to the east, and the balcony to the south. A door is between the
room and the Mage guild to the north.
name: A Simple Livingroom

desc:
With its good location to the south and delicately ornated iron railing, this
balcony should be a relaxing place to sit and watch as the calm waters of the
river Bruinen passes by. Farmland and patches of green meadow dominates the
landscape and the forested sides of the valley confines the view. A light, warm
breeze smelling of burning wood comes from a small doorway to the west. A thin
but sturdy table stands here, in front of a small and simple wooden chair.
name: Balcony Overlooking the Bruinen

desc:
A thin curtain separates the bedchamber from the living room. Like in the rest
of this modest dwelling, it is high up in the arched ceiling and the walls have
no paint. The floor is covered in a light blue colour and there is a colourful
rag carpet that looks a bit misplaced here, maybe a gift from a close friend. A
well-used oil lamp can be seen on a black bedside table and the bed itself
stands along a short side of the room.
name: Tindomerel's Bedchamber

desc:
This passage is made of bright wood as well as being brightly lit by several
bronze lanterns. The walls left and right are made of parallel planks raised one
upon the other and made from teak and fir. The planks extend from the floor to
the ceiling where they form a dome. Several different shaped bricks form from
teak, fir, oak and pine creates an intricate pattern across the dome ceiling.
Looking closer you can recognise the shapes as various incarnations of Eru.
Southward the passage widens into a large hall. To the north lies the Cleric
Guild, where Rivendell's old priest teaches his art.
name: Dome-shaped Passage

desc:
Candles and lanterns light this huge hall which is built of dark oak. The whole
eastern wall has a strip form of bright pine and centred below the strip is an
oilpainting of Elestir. Under the painting is a cosy fireplace and a comfortable
chair. Besides the fireplace a dark door can be seen. From the midst of the
southern wall an able teaken table stretches to the centre of the room, and
along the table wooden chairs are found. Swords and shields of different origin
are carefully hung on the western wall. To the north a long passage can be seen.
name: Bright Oakhall

desc:
This room centers around a very dark wooden altar. The altar seems to be made of
one teak block, there being no lines or cracks indicating the use multiple
blocks. On the altar several books are scattered around, all which are about
ancient magical items. Some notes have also been left here, none of which make
any sense. Even though the altar dominates the room, some other items can be
seen scattered about. Along the northern wall stands a small bed with a nicely
carved bedtable beside it. The bookshelf over the bed is filled with books, some
of them old and dusty others new and bound in leather. A dark door can be found
in western wall.
name: Elestir's Prayroom

desc:
A sturdy wooden ladder rests here attached to the wall, ready to be moved into
position to allow an easy climb down to the old study below. Pictures hang on
the wall here, painted recently. A nice little carpet lies on the ground,
blazing with bright colours of blue and red.
name: Entrance

desc:
Plantlife sits here along the sides of this room, lit by the sun during the day.
Large plates of glass mix in with the wooden ceiling allowing rays of sun to
warm the room throughout the day. There's a small ladder leading down into the
bedroom.
name: A Quaint Living Room

desc:
You find yourself inside a small room surrounded by walls of a blue colour. The
air is fresh and clean and the bedroom is brightly lit. Along the wall is a
wooden desk with a bookshelf beside it, free of dust.
name: A Small Bedroom

desc:
A small path leads through the pine forest and bends to follow the slope high in
the Bruinen valley. It winds past the tall trees, as if those who made it tried
to avoid cutting the wood. At a few places the path meets small openings which
look recently cleared.
name: Path Bordered by Pine Trees

desc:
The path travels along the northern slope of the Bruinen valley. South of the
path the slope is dominated by vineyards, where Elrond's elves grow grapes for
fine wines. There are more vineyards north of the path, but they look abandoned.
High above, the slope is topped by the dark and dense pine forest that hides
Rivendell from outsiders. Some pine trees have been planted along the path to
form an alley when they grow taller.
name: Path Bordered by Pine Trees

desc:
The path leads past a few ancient pines guarding the northern slope of the
Bruinen valley. South of the path, vineyards and the Last Homely Home with its
magnificent elven architecture can be seen, and north of the path the vineyards
continue. A dark forest above the vineyards marks the border of this hidden
valley. A small shelter with six lancet arches supporting a decorated roof has
been constructed here to provide cover from the sun or harsh weather.
name: Path Bordered by Pine Trees

desc:
The path widens and splits in two to enclose a small, round lawn. A circle of
tall pine trees surrounds the lawn and some wooden benches have been placed
underneath. In the middle of the lawn stands a statue of a great hunter. This
small park offers some view over the roofs of the Last Homely Home and the river
Bruinen below.
name: Small Park among the Vineyards

desc:
The small gravel path leads through the northern side of the Bruinen valley. It
travels along the slope to the east and west, whereas some smaller paths lead up
or down into the nearby vineyards. Several thick pine trees protect the path
from harsh winds. A small shelter, built in the typical elven style with lancet
arches and a vaulted roof, invites the traveller to rest and offers a pleasant
view down to the river Bruinen flowing far below.
name: Path Bordered by Pine Trees

desc:
The path ascends towards the east while following the northern slope of the
Bruinen valley. Vineyards have been cultivated along the path, as this side of
the hidden valley receives the most sunlight. High above the vineyards, the dark
outline of a forest marks the border of Rivendell. Some ancient pine trees stand
closer by as if guarding the path.
name: Path Bordered by Pine Trees

desc:
Leading through the vineyards, this small gravel path winds up the northern
slope of the Bruinen valley. It climbs the valley side along a carefully built
serpentine route. A railing protects those who travel the path from slipping and
sliding down the slope. Some small benches have been set at the bends of the
path for tired travellers and those who want to enjoy the wide view over the
valley and the rushing Bruinen far below.
name: Steep Path through the Vineyards

desc:
This is a small yet simple abode, with a white marble altar placed at the
northern corner of the room. A grey, indistinct carpet covers the stone floor,
marking the walkway from the entrance of this chamber to the altar itself. A few
lanterns have been set in small niches alongside the walls, providing a
permanent light source, yet otherwise the walls are grey and bare.
name: Kazadoe's Praying Room

desc:
A great white altar catches the eye of those entering the hall, yet it is
littered with several burnt-out candles. A grey carpet covers most of the wooden
floor, with a few chairs placed in the south-eastern corner, surrounding a small
table. A few lanterns have been placed inside several small niches in the walls,
providing ample light.
name: Mendez' Hall of Healing

desc:
An elegant pavilion has been build on the top of a majestic tree amongst its
numerous leafy branches with the shape of the floor moulding along with the
shape of the tree. The ceiling is made with multi-coloured glass depicting the
city of Rivendell. A brightly coloured deck can be seen protruding to the east
with its rails blooming with flowering vines.
name: A Pavilion

desc:
Light coloured planks of pinewood form a small home, although it is crowded with
many bookshelves and closets. One closet is wide open, showing a wide array of
dress robes of every colour imaginable. The bookshelves are crammed with various
books as well, with every here and there an open spot, as those books have been
laid open upon a small table in the centre of the room. In the northeastern
corner, a small bed has been erected, a cushion of soft leaves upon it.
name: Maverick's Sanctuary

desc:
A rather messy room smelling faintly of lavender opens up from gates made of an
unnaturally strong ivy. An air of power pulsates from a vanity along the eastern
wall. In front of it sits a white marble bench, which seems to be the only clean
item in this entire room. The owner of this house is obviously very fond of
herself, judging by the fourteen portraits covering the remaining wall space. A
small bed sits in the corner, covered in clothes and shoes.
name: A White Chamber

desc:
You find yourself in a secluded part of the clearing. Silglin, the Alchemist
from Lothlorien, was granted this area by Elrond to complete his studies and
experiments. Various items - most of them magical - are stored in chests, pegs,
racks, cabinets and two big desks, which are magically shielded from being
opened by anyone but Silglin himself.
name: Silglin's Laboratory

desc:
The sweet aroma of jasmine after the rain fills this round pavilion with a
serene atmosphere. Huge, masterfully cut granite slabs alternate between
pale-white and slate-black as they cover the perfectly circle floor. In the
centre sits a majestic fountain of an indescribably beautiful priestess pouring
blessings of peace and love from her hands.
name: Angie's Dwelling Place

desc:
Built in a blue-gray stone, the walls to the north are filled with windows
looking out at the mountains. There is a tiny balcony to the north, with a chair
and table on it. Along the south edge of the room, a set of rice paper screens
partially obscure a bed covered in purple velvet. Easels stand around the room
displaying paintings, one of which is clearly unfinished. To the left of the
entrance is a small alcove with its own door and window, where horses can be
stabled temporarily. A writing desk adorns the south wall, with several pieces
of half-finished poetry and letters upon it.
name: Fantasy's Xanadu

desc:
This secluded study is decorated with deep oaken accents; shelves, desks and
chairs are arranged to provide all guests all manner of comfort. A multitude of
scrolls, journals, books and various other forms of writing instruments are
neatly organised throughout the library. One can feel the arcane energy that
resides in this arcane accomodation.
name: Tanmh's Study of Solitude

desc:
Modelled after the council chamber, this room contains a long table with many
comfortable chairs surrounding it. Here the Istari meet informally to discuss
matters that concern them.
name: Istari Council Room

desc:
This bedchamber is richly decorated. The stone walls have several paintings
hanging on them, each depicting the same person. The ground is entirely covered
with a thick, purple carpet. Lavishly decorated chairs are lined up around a
table, a dark red sheet covering it. A wooden bed has been set up in the eastern
corner of the bedchamber, filled with dried leaves.
name: Sjivi's Royal Bedchamber

desc:
Grey walls outline the hall, partially covered with bookshelves littered with
books on the western wall and small paintings of various landscapes on the
opposite side. A few niches have been embedded in the wall with lanterns in
them, lighting the room. A small table stands in a corner, with various
instruments upon it.
name: Naron's Mansion

desc:
Several torches hang all along the walls, lighting a room full of ancient books,
scrolls, maps and unrecognisable, strangely interesting items. The many
bookshelves are filled to the brim, with an occasional book lying on the grey
carpet below. One table stands in the middle, entirely covered with a myriad of
different books and papers, on which illegible scribbles have been written down,
surrounded by many ink smudges.
name: Wabbly's Wizard Keep

desc:
This small house was built from some white-grey stone, with thick oaken beams
supporting the roof. A large window to the south opens up towards the Bruinen
valley and an inspiring vista. The house is sparsely decorated with simple and
sturdy furniture and few decorative items, with a large tapestry hanging on the
eastern wall, depicting some ancient battle by the elves; seemingly, this is the
only item of any larger value.
name: A Small House

desc:
This is where Gelmir returns to rest after his forays into Arda. The walls are
made of a light brown wood, and many fine rugs of blue and silver lie on the
floor. Instead of furniture, squashy cushions made of shimmering fabric dot the
domed and relatively high room. Even though this room lies within a vineyard,
the air in this round chamber smells of fresh winds near the sea.
name: Gelmir's Meditation Chamber

desc:
The entrance leads a bit down and all around are thick branches and heavy, green
leaves. All walls and the roof here seem to be one single, thick bush. A bed
indicates this room gets used for sleeping and even when the roof, consisting of
a thick layer of interwoven branches and leaves, seems waterproof, this room is
quite moist and warm. The result is a thick, white fog that reaches over the
knee of an elf and that completely hides the floor. Rarely something strange,
like a magic fizzle or spark, seems to be felt.
name: Corvax' Foggy House

desc:
This little hut is made entirely out of wood, the floor from simple wooden
planks and the walls from oaken beams. A large window is carved out of the
southern wall, giving a nice view over the Bruinen valley. A wooden table and
chairs stand in front of the window, making it a good place to rest and
philosophise over life. A small and dirty bed stands in the north-west corner,
covered by dirty robes and pointy hats. A wide shelf covers the eastern wall,
filled with old scrolls, strange brews, and books of magic and wizardry.
name: Crazy Wizard's Hut

desc:
This terrace was made from white stones. The views are partly obscured by the
tips of the large, green bushes and slender trees that grow next to the Last
Homely Home. The low balustrade is made of the same white stone and shows many
different, very detailed patterns that resemble deliquesce branches and leaves.
A narrow stair leads down towards a wide ledge above the Bruinen, while a wider
one in the east leads a bit upwards to the Balcony of the Sunset.
name: A Graceful Terrace

desc:
This is the western end of the stone-laid balcony. The precisely laid stones
that form the walkway come to an end in the north, at the beginning of a stone
stairway. The edge of the balcony is made of white stone bearing etchings of
leaves that circle its supporting posts. In the west, a wide but rather short
stair leads a bit down to a white terrace.
name: Balcony of the Sunset

desc:
This corner of the balcony stands above the River Bruinen and the bridge that
crosses it. When looking down over the white stone edge of the balcony, one can
often catch a glimpse of the glimmering waters, running on their course under
the ornately arched bridge. To the east the balcony continues, leading past many
doorways on its way.
name: Bridge Overlook

desc:
Near the end of the path stands a white, mostly overgrown pavilion that is known
as the Last Pavilion or the Pavilion of the Bruinen. Not that far below lies the
river and the Pier of the Bruinen, while the part of the Last Homely Home that
is visible just to the north and a bit above, is covered with dense vines.
name: At the Last Pavilion

desc:
You enter a brightly lit room, with candles all over. The walls are covered with
bookshelves, filled with ancient, dusty books about priests' knowledge. Along
the southern wall you see a thick oaken desk covered with notes, featherpens and
books. Along the western shelf a small, yet cosy bed stands, ready for recovery.
name: Kaldae's Room of Prayer

desc:
A hollow space in the tree-trunk leads upward into the swaying branches
overhead. A system of ropes has been strung between the larger limbs, creating a
soft floor which sways with the branches in the breeze. On the western side, a
modest bed is covered with a sky-blue blanket, with a large oilcloth nearby in
case of inclement weather. A small bureau anchored to the trunk holds writing
and drawing utensils. The hollow base of the trunk has just enough room to
stable a horse temporarily. From up here there is a lovely view of the sky and
surrounding mountains, and a gentle breeze stirs branches and leaves in a
peaceful natural symphony.
name: Gail's Hideout among the Swaying Branches

desc:
This old dwelling is located inside a dense thicket of entangled vines. It has
been decorated with a few simple furnitures, and a magnificient pelt of an elk
has been set on the eastern wall. It is hard to tell whether this room is still
in regular use; an ancient smell of wood coming from the old furnitures and from
the walls suggests no, yet the room is surprisingly tidy and clean, and its air
is filled with strange, small sparklings radiating bright light.
name: Shambler's Hideout behind the Vines

desc:
A path runs along the southern side of a cliff overlooking the valley of the
Bruinen. A set of stairs leads upward toward the Last Pavilion. A majestic
latticework faces this staircase, preventing any climber from falling down the
corbelling of the path. The rock wall to the north is very steep, and the only
way up is the set of stairs carved into its surface. The flank of the cliff is
made of a white stone flecked with countless black spots. A delicately carved
trellis along the path supports numerous climbing plants.
name: At the Bottom of a Steep Staircase

desc:
This bridge links two parts of a collapsed cliff on the north side of the river
Bruinen. Just below the bridge, the mass of rock that would be the missing
connection lies in the water, forming a reef around which water flows in small
rapids. The bridge is covered by an arched roof of white tile; to either side of
it, there are white marble railings overgrown with wild plants. A slight recess
in the cliff wall enables a glimpse of the Last Homely Home which lies at the
top of the cliff.
name: On a Graceful Bridge

desc:
A path runs along a natural ledge that was carved by wind and rain. The passage
is sheltered by the stone mass of a cliff above it, which provides a natural
roof. A low railing, dark in colour to match the walls, has been added for
safety. A dozen statues are carved into the wall, each bearing great detail in
their solemn gaze over the river below. The smoothness of the carvings and the
alcoves that host them match perfectly with the well-maintained path. Below and
to the south, the Bruinen laps on the rock, producing a soft melody.
name: A Ledge above the Bruinen

desc:
A very narrow path runs beneath an overhanging mass of stone. The ceiling is
delicately decorated with floral patterns sculpted into the rock. The ground has
been carved to yield the smoothest possible surface to walk on. In a number of
places along the path, there are shallow alcoves which might make excellent
places for an artisan to isolate themselves and work while contemplating the
Bruinen valley that stretches out below.
name: A Narrow Ledge over the Bruinen

desc:
A broad path forms a natural terrace overhanging the Bruinen to the south.
Passing years have gathered a wide variety of plants to this unique environment
which is isolated and exposed to the weather. The result is a natural garden
where vegetables grow freely in a simple harmony exalted by the colours of many
blossoms. A small depression at the foot of a cliff stores rainwater for all
that lives here. The peace of the garden is occasionally disturbed by the
clamour of a pier on the Bruinen which lies to the east.
name: A Natural Garden

desc:
A wide, flat shelf protrudes from the side of a cliff, forming a natural balcony
over the river Bruinen. A paved path snakes along the shelf, bending left and
right to fit the curves and recesses of the cliff wall. A wooden roof covers
this balcony, and at the edge of the shelf there is a railing made of delicately
carved marble which perfectly matches the white pavement. A steep and narrow
marble staircase plunges down towards a small house at the water level.
name: On a Balcony over the Bruinen

desc:
Thick tangles of vegetation have taken overtaken this path, joining vines that
cover the wide, steep cliff to the north. Though the plants grow wild and
untended, the vegetation forms five arches over the trail, meeting a railing
that runs along a ledge overlooking the Bruinen. The path broadens to the west,
and the cliff wall begins to descend to meet it.
name: The Path over the Bruinen

desc:
A path runs uphill and downhill over a series of gentle slopes. To the west, it
skirts a rocky spur too rugged to traverse. On the ground, smooth gravel mixes
with short grass that softly dampens any footsteps. To the north, a lovely and
colourful plain occupies a slight depression.
name: A Slopy Trail over the Bruinen

desc:
This plain sits in a wide depression between slopes which rise to the west and
to the east. Numerous, varied blossoms grow in a natural and pleasant
hotchpotch. There are a few cottages on the plain; the residents likely benefit
from the cool air in the vicinity of the Bruinen as well as the contemplative
remoteness of the vale. A lush grove grows to the north.
name: A Plain near the Last Homely Home

desc:
Inside a tight circle of trees, an elven home has been built between the trunks,
and on the branches of, living trees. The branches have skilfully been shaped
into furniture; there are sturdy branches to sit upon, a wooden plate set as a
table surface upon a natural framework, and entwined slender boughs forming a
narrow bed above. On the ground, a small spring bubbles clear water.
name: Tarian's Living room

desc:
This torch-lit chamber offers a sense of serenity for the homeowner and her
guests. The Noldo glyphed hardwood floor symbolizes the race Asako is known for.
A bookshelf constructed of red-oak is placed against the wall facing towards the
bed, holding a multitude of scrolls. A comfortable stone bench is located next
to the bookshelf where guests may be seated. A four pillar king bed is situated
next to the open window where cool air entises the room. At the other end of the
room, a statue of Rogon is overlooking the door. Jewels and scalps of enemies
are placed on the pedestal as an offering to Rogon to whom Asako fights for.
name: Asako's Resting Chamber

desc:
The spacious hallway runs east to west. On the red velvet-covered walls you
notice some portraits of ancient elves, whose exploits during the first age of
Middle-earth are now legend; Fingolfin, high king of the Noldor elves, Maedhros,
the most famous son of Feanor, and Thingol Greycloak, Sinda king of Doriath.
name: Grand Hallway

desc:
Fresh pine needles are strewn about the wooden floor, adding both a pleasant
aroma and a natural filter for bugs. A few open windows add fresh air and extra
light to the room, often disturbing the needles and creating patches of bare
floor. An oak cabinet against the northern wall is half-open, its contents
visible to all. A small ornamental chair and matching table offer small comfort
to the room's inhabitants.
name: Diane's Room

desc:
This is a simple wooden structure located between two large wooden branches. The
treehouse is modest and uncluttered with windows on every wall, enabling natural
light to enter. A rather unusual feature is the roof overhead that appears
almost transparent. A simple carpet covers the wooden floor, often used for
meditations.
name: Budach's Treehouse

desc:
Thick hedges accompany a meandering path that follows the Bruinen river, which
continues in a general east-west fashion. The path curves quite often, making
way for random boulders, budding trees and thick bushes. Some of the bushes are
quite thorny, but have been trimmed in order to limit exposure.
name: Meandering Path along the Bruinen

desc:
A soft path runs alongside the Bruinen river. Lush grass grows all around,
interspersed with a few cottages of various sizes that have a perfect view of
the river; some with small gardens attached and some with a small orchard
instead. An oak tree stands tall between two of the cottages; once the seedling
of the massive oak noticed to the far west perhaps, with another small cottage
being held aloft by large branches. Several bushes accompany the path and all
are neatly trimmed, providing a source of food for the local wildlife.
name: Meandering Path along the Bruinen

desc:
In a quiet section of Rivendell stands this humble home. While modest in size,
the grand views of the valley impress beyond imagination. Flowerbeds surround
the windows filling the air with a sweet fragrance. A small bed sits under the
south window which frames the cascading waterfall above. On the west wall a
simple kitchen lined with cupboards catches the eye. The cupboards are filled
with delicate plates and glasses of the highest quality.
name: Muezza's Sanctuary

desc:
In a silent corner of the winding building is a surprisingly spacious abode,
perfect for a small bed. The room is decorated sparsely with a handful of maps,
many of which are faded and hardly recognisable due to heavy usage. A bookshelf,
worn at the edges as if itself had once been read from cover to cover, is the
other singular piece of furniture. A tidy and clean atmosphere sets the
surroundings. A selection of aged, yet clean rugs, slightly mismatched in colour
have been placed carefully across the floor, small areas of pine appearing among
the gaps, creating the impression of wooden slats between the fading colours.
Cutting across the ceiling is a single bolt of lightning, carved into the
granite roof, appearing to lack the deliberate care of the rest of the room.
name: Djoko's Chamber

desc:
This room is the bedchamber of half-elven Taramikir. The room itself has precise
quadrate dimensions, its walls are high and made out of mahogany from the forest
of Fangorn. Burgundy-coloured bookshelves fill the western side of the room,
facing two enormous portraits adjacent to the door. The enchanted glass floor
emits a bright, glowing light, creating a warm and cosy atmosphere.
name: Taramikir's Condo

desc:
Crushed pebbles form a path in the garden leading from the east, then curving to
the south where it leads to a small hut. Flowers grow along the path in vibrant
colours and various sizes. A few bushes grow on the northern side of the garden,
behind a small rock garden. The rocks and stones all have a shiny finish and
glisten.
name: Mangkubumi's Garden

desc:
This particular room looks like a study, with a small desk and matching chair
standing in one corner, covered with hundreds of pieces of paper with varying
degrees of unintelligable scribbles. The faint odour of something burning
lingers, although no flame or smoke are visible. The frame of a bed stands on
the opposite side of the desk, relatively clear of debris, with a small feather
mattress and blanket.
name: Thiwzan's Study Room

desc:
As you step into the circular entryway, you notice the elegant marble floor and
the golden lamps that adorn the walls. Lining the north wall are chairs carved
from rich mahogany and a polished oak bookshelf that is filled with books
written about the adventures of Zaber. To the south is a stone fireplace that
gives off a quiet warmth.
name: Grand Entryway

desc:
You emerge onto an open patio that is lit by softly glowing lamps. The lamps sit
upon stands of oak, inlaid with patterns of mithril. In the centre of the patio
is a small, round table surrounded by chairs of mahogany. A railing of brightly
polished steel surrounds the patio, and below you can see the beautiful
courtyard of Rivendell.
name: Patio above the Courtyard

desc:
Eight broad pillars of pale marble form a private octagon-shaped sanctuary
offering relaxation from the hasty world outside. Twinned, silvery linings are
attached around the top of the pillars, connecting them with each other.
Twisting above the polished floor of stone, grey-green vines ensnare the
strings, and like a roof they dazzle with their flowery shrubs as a few lit
candles illuminate the surroundings. The sparkling noise of the fountain in the
centre of the room seems to diminish as a disappearing shadow uncovers a
painting of a black, grandeur ship that sails under the flag of Numenor. The
royal bloodline, daubed in gold, spans vertically beneath the shimmering blue
surface of water.
name: Tindomiel's Garden of Twilight

desc:
Darkness fills the room, only broken by the sputtering light of dying torches.
The room is circular, with many oaken shelves nailed haphazardly throughout. On
the shelves, there are various magical instruments as well as some unidentified
iron-shod weights. In the dead centre of the room, there is an old and abused
couch, surrounded by books, drawings, and mysteriously humming objects.
name: Rova's Legendarium

desc:
A glass room built high and nestled onto several branches of a large oak tree
where Chezkla and his friends hang out and study arcane mysteries while enjoying
the view. With amazing attention to detail the weather never affects the comfort
inside, only the view changes. One day it is the dancing Bruinen, the next, it
is the flowing beauty of the clouds. In one corner is a homely bed for
refreshing sleep.
name: Chezkla's Conservatory

desc:
The walls of this chamber are rough hewn stone, chipped haphazardly out of the
surrounding walls. A soft, green glow can be seen permeating the room, but the
source cannot be determined. Daggers, crossbows, and bows are shoved in the
corners, with a giant table stacked with scrolls taking up the majority of the
space. A family lineage map is carved into the stone walls, showing the line of
Feanor. The last entry on the map shows the name Fea, decorated with a sinuous
snake.
name: The Chamber of Secrets

desc:
This is magically invented abode. The enchanted ceiling changes its shape biased
on running time from a lavender blue sky with the golden beam, giving the room a
natural light during day, to a dark blue of night with inner silver moonshine
and thousands of fine stars. The flooring looks simply - a lawn, but it is as
nice as cozy. A semi-large mahagony bed, embellished with fine jewels of
multi-colours, stands at the western wall. Among it, fixed to the wall, is a
pine shelf with miscellaneous sort of herbs, scrolls and potions. Opposite it is
a large window with enchanted glass, giving a clear view of the outside area.
Right of it is an oak cabinet filled with fashioned clothes, magical artifacts,
rings and necklaces. Left of the window is a tall bookcase carrying widely very
old looking books. In the middle of the northern wall stands a grated fireplace,
which gives a fine warm to the room. The southeastern corner gives a huge space
for a weapon racks and armour made of shiny unknown metal. An oak doorway leads
southwards to the balcony.
name: Kaka's Royal Bedchamber

desc:
This comfortable room seems alive due to the walls being covered in a manner of
all green, growing plants that thrive with the love and care of an Elf. Vines
and ivy cover the walls, their slender tendrils dance into the air as one moves
past them. The room is lit by crystal lamps in the corners, and a cosy bed that
stands alongside one wall; promising the comfort for the owner of this home.
Several thick vines have been intertwined to be used as a ladder that leads up
into the room above.
name: Jaak's Treehouse among the Living Vines

desc:
The room is small and square, with a high ceiling. The walls are made of thick
vines, covered in thorns. The air is damp and musty, and the air whistling
between gaps in the vines sounds vaguely of screams. The walls move ever so
slightly in and out, as if the breathing of a sleeping nightmare. In regular
intervals are sets of smaller vines affixed to the walls. The floor is covered
in a thick layer of moss. Here and there a large thorn pokes through the moss
almost in an attempt to trip those unaware. The only light comes from a very
small porthole high up on the wall with a criss-cross of vines preventing
egress. In the centre of the room is a single chair made of vines and the seat
is a mesh of soft moss. Blocking the only point of exit is a large, thick wooden
door. Stretching across the door from each direction are large vines as slide
locks, preventing passage.
name: Tie's Living Dungeon

desc:
The room is simple. One cannot see much furniture inside - merely a bed, a chair
and a table. Further investigation reveals several drawings, maps, and an array
of weapons, filling the walls of this dimly lit lodge. Nothing else can be seen,
only an old carpet covering the floor.
name: Tatanka's Chamber

desc:
This is a small and yet simple abode, with a white marble altar placed at the
northern corner of the room. A grey, indistinct carpet covers the stone floor,
marking the walkway from the entrance of this chamber to the altar itself. A few
lanterns have been set in small niches alongside the walls, providing a
permanent light source, yet otherwise the walls are grey and bare.
name: Khamr's Praying Room

desc:
Once you stepped into this room, a warm presence surrounded your body. The
circular room is large and beautiful. There is no furniture but a round table
and four chairs in the centre of the room. The solid walls of soft, polished oak
are without windows. The light comes from a mysterious, shimmering bowl of stone
on the table. The blue glow is very bright and fills the room like a clear,
transparent cloud.
name: A Blue Room

desc:
Healthy grass reminiscent of a well-tended lawn covers this small opening under
some tall pines. There are no large rocks or gnarled roots in the ground, making
this seem like a great place for building a small house. A narrow footpath leads
to the trail at some distance to the east.
name: Clearing among the Pine Trees

desc:
Veiled in twilight, this quiet sanctum exudes an air of reverence. Smooth, pale
stone arches rise overhead, filigreed with silver traceries that catch the dim
glow of a crescent-shaped lamp suspended at its centre. A great arched window to
the north, veiled in gauzy drapery, allows the scent of evening jasmine and
distant rain to drift inside. Beneath it all, a rug of deep sapphire spans the
floor, embroidered with celestial patterns. To the south, a polished oak door
inlaid with strands of truesilver leads beyond. To the west, a curved archway,
framed in pale stone and entwined with silver-leafed ivy, opens into a quiet
garden where moonlight lingers.
name: Moonlit Sanctum

desc:
This round room has no windows, no doors, and no exits. The room is deep within
Rivendell and is where the Elven folk send the malcontents, the rule-breakers,
and those in need of reflection on their misdeeds. This austere room contains no
distractions and provides a safe place for self-reflection.
name: The Introspection Room
